title: Crepidophyllum wildii
taxonomy: Crepidophyllum wildii is classified as a member of the genus Crepidophyllum and the family Selaginellaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which consists of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. More specifically, Crepidophyllum wildii is a species of spikemoss, a group of plants that are known for their small size and delicate foliage. The Selaginellaceae family is a group of spikemosses that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Crepidophyllum wildii is a unique and fascinating member of this family, with characteristics that set it apart from other species of spikemoss.
uses: Crepidophyllum wildii is not a well-known plant in the English-speaking world but a genus of extinct, fossilized plants. However, there is a genus called Crepidophyllum, which belongs to the ferns. Some species of ferns, in particular, the genus Osmunda (Royal Fern) are used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. intro:
There is limited information available on the plant Crepidophyllum wildii. However, if you would like to learn about a similar plant, Crepidophyllum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Selaginellaceae. 

The genus Selaginella, to which Crepidophyllum is related, includes the plant Selaginella, which is often referred to as Spikemoss. 

Selaginella species have been reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.

Some of the bioactive constituents of Selaginella species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Selaginella species include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
